title: ICE Opens Perjury Probe After Agents Made False Statements Under Oath About Minneapolis Shooting
excerpt: ICE Director Todd Lyons announced that two unnamed ICE officers had been placed on administrative leave after a joint ICE–DOJ review of video evidence revealed their sworn testimony about the January 14 Minneapolis shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis was materially false. All charges against Sosa-Celis and his cousin were simultaneously dismissed with prejudice.
content:
On February 13, 2026, acting ICE Director Todd Lyons disclosed that federal authorities had opened a criminal perjury investigation into two ICE officers following the collapse of charges against two Venezuelan men in connection with a Minneapolis shooting.

  • A joint ICE–DOJ review of video evidence revealed that 'sworn testimony provided by two separate officers appears to have made untruthful statements' about the January 14 encounter in which agent shot Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis in the thigh — contradicting DHS's earlier assertion that officers were ambushed with a snow shovel and broom handle.
  • Newly released surveillance footage showed the confrontation lasted approximately 12 seconds — not the 'three-minute struggle' described by officers — and that a shovel visible in the footage was dropped on the ground before any physical contact began.
  • Federal prosecutors had access to the surveillance footage within hours of the shooting but reportedly did not review it until nearly three weeks after filing charges against the two men. Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said 'bare due diligence would have shown that the agents were lying.'
  • The two officers face potential termination and criminal prosecution pending the outcome of the U.S. Attorney's investigation.

The reversal followed a string of incidents in which DHS's characterizations of use-of-force events were contradicted by bystander video and local witness accounts, deepening a credibility crisis for the agency.
